ops.text:
{"config":{"cells":["e3_both","e3_hard","e3_random","e6_w1","e6_w3","e6_w6","encoder_ettin150m","encoder_modernbert","fewshot_medium","fewshot_small","lexical"],"constraint":"latency_ms_single <= 50 for always-on","leaderboard":"results/E1/leaderboard.md","primary_metric":"pr_auc_frozen_eval","report":"results/E1/report.json","summary":true,"tiebreak":"recall_at_2per100_viewratio_proxy","winner":"lexical","winner_reason":"ok"},"config_hash":"e076e8cbe232","dataset_digest":"cc007756a4c54ff8","experiment":"E1-gate-bakeoff","metrics":{"n_cells":11.0,"pr_auc__e3_both":0.9361200458970723,"pr_auc__e3_hard":0.9286034614885359,"pr_auc__e3_random":0.918050502401089,"pr_auc__e6_w1":0.9174361993174339,"pr_auc__e6_w3":0.9268587728024721,"pr_auc__e6_w6":0.9361200458970723,"pr_auc__encoder_ettin150m":0.869918296455239,"pr_auc__encoder_modernbert":0.8673840586899921,"pr_auc__fewshot_medium":0.8458074216537727,"pr_auc__fewshot_small":0.8200779834173927,"pr_auc__lexical":0.9361200458970723,"winner_latency_ms":8.163802930503152,"winner_pr_auc":0.9361200458970723},"seed":1729,"verdict":"**Lexical wins Stage-1.** The TF-IDF (word 1-2g + char 3-5g) + balanced-LR baseline tops the board at
PR-AUC 0.9361 on the frozen eval — a full 6.6 points over both fine-tuned encoders (ModernBERT-base
0.8674, Ettin-150m 0.8699 under the 20-min budget: 5,500 rows, 1,024 tail tokens, 1 epoch) and far above
the few-shot LLM judges (sonnet 0.8458, haiku 0.8201 on the 250-row subset, vs a ~0.80 positive base
rate) — at 8.2 ms/example, the only family comfortably inside the 50 ms always-on constraint, and it is
the only cell that spends the whole 2-fires/100-rows alert budget on true positives
(recall 0.0246 = the 27/1099 ceiling; both encoders score 0.0000 there — their most confident fires are
all false positives, which disqualifies them for a tight-budget gate today despite far better calibration,
ECE 0.073-0.075 vs lexical's 0.281). Two caveats travel with the win: the coefficients show it partly
keys on format artifacts (`1mopus`/`1msonnet` ANSI-escape remnants, `compact user`, `13585ch`-style
counters), so it is brittle to prompt/format drift, and every family is weak on `transcript_message`
positives (lexical 0.746, encoders 0.60-0.62) — the gate as-exported mostly learns `question_answer`-style
windows. **As a load-shedding prefilter in front of a heavier stage, lexical is NOT currently worth it:**
at a 99%-recall threshold it still passes 97.6% of all rows (~92% of negatives at view ratio), so it sheds
almost nothing — its value is as the Stage-1 scorer itself (near-free at 8 ms/166 rows/s batch), not as a
funnel; revisit only if a Stage-2 with clearly better ranking on the passed set exists, which nothing in
this bake-off provides. **E6 recommendation:** keep the full rewind window — PR-AUC is monotone in W
(0.9174 at W=1, 0.9269 at W=3, 0.9361 at W=6); do not narrow the positive windows. **E3 recommendation:**
keep the mixed negative pool — random+hard (0.9361) beats hard-only (0.9286) beats random-only (0.9181);
hard negatives carry more signal than random ones, but both are needed. All numbers are at the view's
~4:1 positive ratio, not the natural steer rate; budget/recall figures are proxies at that ratio."}
